perturbation.original_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby confirm the agreement by and among them to the joint filing on behalf of them of the Statement on Schedule 13D and any and all further amendments thereto, with respect to the securities of the above referenced issuer, and that this Agreement be included as an Exhibit to such filing.
perturbation.changed_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby confirm the understanding by and among them to the collaborative filing on behalf of them of the Statement on Schedule 13D and any and all further revisions thereto, with respect to the securities of the above referenced issuer, and that this Agreement be conditionally included as an Exhibit to such filing.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'agreement' to 'understanding' and 'amendments' to 'revisions' introduces ambiguity, as the SEC requires a clear, binding agreement for joint filings under Schedule 13D, not merely an 'understanding'. Changing 'included' to 'conditionally included' adds uncertainty.
perturbation.law_explanation: Rule 13d-1(k) mandates a formal, written agreement for joint filings. Replacing 'agreement' with 'understanding' and adding the term 'conditionally' weakens the binding nature required by the SEC, potentially violating the rule.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 240.13d-1 - Filing of Schedules 13D and 13G. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.13d-1 - Filing of Schedules 13D and 13G. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 240.13d-1 Filing of Schedules 13D and 13G. (a) Any person who, after acquiring directly or indirectly the beneficial ownership of any equity security of a class which is specified in paragraph (i)(1) of this section , is directly or indirectly the beneficial owner of more than five percent of the class shall, within five business days after the date of the acquisition, file with the Commission, a statement containing the information required by Schedule 13D (§ 240.13d-101) . (b) (1) A person who would otherwise be obligated under paragraph (a) of this section to file a statement on Schedule 13D (§ 240.13d-101) may, in lieu thereof, file with the Commission, a short-form statement on Schedule 13G (§ 240.13d-102), Provided, That: (i) Such person has acquired such securities in the ordinary course of the person's business and not with the purpose nor with the effect of changing or influencing the control of the issuer , nor in connection with or as a participant in any transaction having such purpose or effect, including any transaction subject to § 240.13d-3(b) and (ii) Such person is: (A) A broker or dealer registered under section 15 of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 78o ); (B) A bank as defined in section 3(a)(6) of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 78c ); (C) An insurance company as defined in section 3(a)(19) of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 78c ); (D) An investment company registered under section 8 of the Investment Company Act of 1940 ( 15 U.S.C. 80a-8 ); (E) Any person registered as an investment adviser under Section 203 of the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 ( 15 U.S.C. 80b-3 ) or under the laws of any state; (F) An employee benefit plan as defined in Section 3(3) of the Employee Retirement Income Security A
perturbation.justification: The change from 'agreement' to 'understanding' and the addition of 'conditionally' weakens the binding nature of the contract as required for joint filings under Schedule 13D. According to the law explanation, Rule 13d-1(k) mandates a formal written agreement. The modified text introduces ambiguity and uncertainty, potentially violating the rule, as 'understanding' lacks the legal force of 'agreement' and 'conditionally included' suggests the exhibit might not always be included. While the provided scraped snippets don't explicitly define 'agreement' in this context, they pertain to filings under Schedule 13D, reinforcing the relevance of the law explanation.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts each of which shall be deemed to be an original and all of which together shall be deemed to constitute one and the same Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This arrangement may be initiated with several copies each of which shall be considered a draft and all of which together shall be considered one and the similar document.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Agreement' to 'arrangement', 'counterparts' to 'copies', 'original' to 'draft', and 'same Agreement' to 'similar document' undermines the legal force of the joint filing agreement. The SEC requires that each counterpart be considered an original to ensure all parties are bound equally.
perturbation.law_explanation: By replacing key terms with weaker synonyms, the contract casts doubt on the legal validity of the joint filing, contradicting the requirement for a binding agreement as defined in Rule 13d-1(k).

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 240.13d-1 - Filing of Schedules 13D and 13G. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.13d-1 - Filing of Schedules 13D and 13G. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 240.13d-1 Filing of Schedules 13D and 13G. (a) Any person who, after acquiring directly or indirectly the beneficial ownership of any equity security of a class which is specified in paragraph (i)(1) of this section , is directly or indirectly the beneficial owner of more than five percent of the class shall, within five business days after the date of the acquisition, file with the Commission, a statement containing the information required by Schedule 13D (§ 240.13d-101) . (b) (1) A person who would otherwise be obligated under paragraph (a) of this section to file a statement on Schedule 13D (§ 240.13d-101) may, in lieu thereof, file with the Commission, a short-form statement on Schedule 13G (§ 240.13d-102), Provided, That: (i) Such person has acquired such securities in the ordinary course of the person's business and not with the purpose nor with the effect of changing or influencing the control of the issuer , nor in connection with or as a participant in any transaction having such purpose or effect, including any transaction subject to § 240.13d-3(b) and (ii) Such person is: (A) A broker or dealer registered under section 15 of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 78o ); (B) A bank as defined in section 3(a)(6) of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 78c ); (C) An insurance company as defined in section 3(a)(19) of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 78c ); (D) An investment company registered under section 8 of the Investment Company Act of 1940 ( 15 U.S.C. 80a-8 ); (E) Any person registered as an investment adviser under Section 203 of the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 ( 15 U.S.C. 80b-3 ) or under the laws of any state; (F) An employee benefit plan as defined in Section 3(3) of the Employee Retirement Income Security A
perturbation.justification: The original text uses specific legal terms ('Agreement', 'counterparts', 'original') that carry legal weight, especially in the context of filings as potentially governed by regulations like 17 CFR § 240.13d-1 (Snippet 2), which discusses filing requirements with the Commission. The changed text weakens these terms by using synonyms ('arrangement', 'copies', 'draft') which undermines the binding nature of the agreement. The Perturbation Explanation highlights that the SEC requires each counterpart to be considered an original. By changing 'original' to 'draft,' the changed text directly contradicts this requirement, which suggests a formal binding agreement is no longer assured, thus creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned hereby execute this Agreement as of February 20, 2020.
perturbation.changed_text: AS A SUGGESTION, the undersigned may consider accepting this memo as of February 20, 2020.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'execute this Agreement' with 'consider accepting this memo' changes the obligatory action to a suggestion, undermining the intent to create a legally binding agreement. The phrase 'In Witness Whereof' is a standard closing in contracts that legally binds signers; removing it and softening the verb weakens the filing and contradicts Securities Exchange Act of 1934 requirements.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification removes the legally binding aspect of the agreement, failing to meet the requirements for a joint filing agreement under Rule 13d-1(k) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 240.13d-1 - Filing of Schedules 13D and 13G. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.13d-1 - Filing of Schedules 13D and 13G. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 240.13d-1 Filing of Schedules 13D and 13G. (a) Any person who, after acquiring directly or indirectly the beneficial ownership of any equity security of a class which is specified in paragraph (i)(1) of this section , is directly or indirectly the beneficial owner of more than five percent of the class shall, within five business days after the date of the acquisition, file with the Commission, a statement containing the information required by Schedule 13D (§ 240.13d-101) . (b) (1) A person who would otherwise be obligated under paragraph (a) of this section to file a statement on Schedule 13D (§ 240.13d-101) may, in lieu thereof, file with the Commission, a short-form statement on Schedule 13G (§ 240.13d-102), Provided, That: (i) Such person has acquired such securities in the ordinary course of the person's business and not with the purpose nor with the effect of changing or influencing the control of the issuer , nor in connection with or as a participant in any transaction having such purpose or effect, including any transaction subject to § 240.13d-3(b) and (ii) Such person is: (A) A broker or dealer registered under section 15 of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 78o ); (B) A bank as defined in section 3(a)(6) of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 78c ); (C) An insurance company as defined in section 3(a)(19) of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 78c ); (D) An investment company registered under section 8 of the Investment Company Act of 1940 ( 15 U.S.C. 80a-8 ); (E) Any person registered as an investment adviser under Section 203 of the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 ( 15 U.S.C. 80b-3 ) or under the laws of any state; (F) An employee benefit plan as defined in Section 3(3) of the Employee Retirement Income Security A
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces obligatory language ('execute this Agreement') with suggestive language ('may consider accepting this memo'), which contradicts the requirement for a legally binding agreement. The perturbation explanation supports this, stating the change undermines the intent to create a legally binding agreement under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. The law explanation also indicates that the modification fails to meet requirements for a joint filing agreement. Scraped snippet 2 references filing requirements under 17 CFR § 240.13d-1, which implies the existence of obligations, unlike the suggestion presented in the modified text. The original text ensures a formal agreement is created by specifying who is agreeing to the contract, whereas the changed text creates a suggestion that no party is actually compelled to follow. Therefore, a legal contradiction exists.
